message.content:
you have to create a shell script called : start.sh , this script will connect to database redpill_dev of localhost , it will select id and name of active websites 
This list will be presented using fzf.
Once the label of the website is selected, the script will export website id in REDPILL_WEBSITE_ID env var and then use symfony local:server:start

input.description: Find Doctrine mapping files for Website
Check Website ORM mapping for table name

content.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<doctrine-mapping
        xmlns="http://doctrine-project.org/schemas/orm/doctrine-mapping"
        xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
        xsi:schemaLocation="http://doctrine-project.org/schemas/orm/doctrine-mapping http://doctrine-project.org/schemas/orm/doctrine-mapping.xsd"
        xmlns:gedmo="http://gediminasm.org/schemas/orm/doctrine-extensions-mapping">

    <entity repository-class="Eliberty\RedpillBundle\Entity\Repository\WebsiteRepository" name="Eliberty\RedpillBundle\Entity\Website" table="websites">
        <id name="id" type="integer" column="id">
            <generator strategy="IDENTITY"/>
        </id>

        <field name="serverName" type="string" column="server_name"  nullable="false"/>
        <field name="altNames" type="string" column="alt_names"  nullable="true"/>

        <field name="createdAt" type="datetime" column="created_at" nullable="false">
            <gedmo:timestampable on="create"/>
        </field>
        <field name="updatedAt" type="datetime" column="updated_at" nullable="false">
            <gedmo:timestampable on="update"/>

content.text: Now I have all the info needed. The table is `websites` with columns `id`, `server_name`, and `active`.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SELECTION=$(psql -h localhost -d redpill_dev -t -A -F $'\t' \
    -c "SELECT id, server_name FROM websites WHERE active = true ORDER BY server_name;" \
    | fzf --prompt="Select website: " --with-nth=2 --delimiter=$'\t')

if [[ -z "$SELECTION" ]]; then
    echo "No website selected, aborting." >&2
    exit 1
fi

WEBSITE_ID=$(echo "$SELECTION" | cut -f1)
WEBSITE_NAME=$(echo "$SELECTION" | cut -f2)

export REDPILL_WEBSITE_ID="$WEBSITE_ID"
echo "Starting server for website: $WEBSITE_NAME (id=$WEBSITE_ID)"

symfony local:server:start

toolUseResult.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SELECTION=$(psql -h localhost -d redpill_dev -t -A -F $'\t' \
    -c "SELECT id, server_name FROM websites WHERE active = true ORDER BY server_name;" \
    | fzf --prompt="Select website: " --with-nth=2 --delimiter=$'\t')

if [[ -z "$SELECTION" ]]; then
    echo "No website selected, aborting." >&2
    exit 1
fi

WEBSITE_ID=$(echo "$SELECTION" | cut -f1)
WEBSITE_NAME=$(echo "$SELECTION" | cut -f2)

export REDPILL_WEBSITE_ID="$WEBSITE_ID"
echo "Starting server for website: $WEBSITE_NAME (id=$WEBSITE_ID)"

symfony local:server:start

content.text:
Done. `start.sh` created and made executable.

Key details:
- Queries `websites` table for `active = true`, ordered by `server_name`
- `fzf` displays only the name (`--with-nth=2`) while keeping the id in the tab-separated row
- Extracts the id from the selection and exports `REDPILL_WEBSITE_ID`
- Then calls `symfony local:server:start`

Run it with `./start.sh`. Note: `psql` will use your current system user for authentication by default — if you need credentials, add `-U <user>` to the psql command.

lastPrompt: you have to create a shell script called : start.sh , this script will connect to database redpill_dev of localhost , it will select id and name of active websites  This list will be presented using f…
